summary: Seven proposals targeting unused signal dimensions — listing age (coin maturity), day_change_pct as oversold context, negative premium for spot-led demand, negative funding as short-crowding proxy, global_short_ratio_high + OI confirmation for squeeze setup, small-cap volume burst, and oi_pct_top_decile (historical level vs delta) — all verified against SUPPORTED_FIELDS and each combining two or more orthogonal conditions.

proposals.notes: Buy pump signal on coins already down 5%+ on the day where fresh taker buying confirms oversold reversal rather than exhaustion continuation.
proposals.rationale: day_change_pct as an oversold-context filter has not been combined with event_type=pump + taker_buy_ratio_5m in any existing entry; distinct from pure momentum entries that fire regardless of prior-day context.

proposals.notes: OI surge on coins listed within 6 months with taker buying confirmation; newer listings have fewer sophisticated arbitrageurs so OI pumps more likely to translate to price follow-through.
proposals.rationale: listing_age_days is a completely unused dimension across all 523 existing archetypes; pairing coin maturity with OI surge is structurally distinct from any existing filter combination.

proposals.notes: Pump event when global short ratio is at high-percentile threshold and OI is building — crowded short positioning plus new longs entering creates a forced-covering cascade.
proposals.rationale: global_short_ratio_high flag has not been gated by oi_change_pct growth confirmation; E147/E148 used isolated global_long/short fields without OI growth, making this structurally distinct with two orthogonal squeeze signals.

proposals.notes: Pump signal when funding is meaningfully negative (shorts paying longs) with aggressive taker buying — short-crowded perpetual plus spot-side demand pressure signals high squeeze probability.
proposals.rationale: Negative funding as a directional filter (shorts crowded) is distinct from funding_abs_high (magnitude-only); E154/E155 targeted Reserved6 channel and crashed on no_events; this uses pricechange channel with a confirmed-supported operator condition.

proposals.notes: OI surge with perp trading at discount to spot (negative premium) plus taker buying — real spot demand driving the move rather than derivative-led speculation, suggesting higher-quality pump.
proposals.rationale: premium_bps in negative direction for longs is unexplored; E181 used premium_diverge for shorts and E187/E188 used basis_align/diverge; negative premium on a long entry targets the spot-demand-led regime, which is structurally opposite.

proposals.notes: Pump on a small-cap coin with historically extreme volume plus taker buying confirmation; thin liquidity combined with a volume surge amplifies pump follow-through probability.
proposals.rationale: marketcap_bucket=small combined with volume_pct_top_decile is a novel size-plus-liquidity-burst pairing; E051 used liquidity_bucket=high which implicitly selects larger caps — this targets the opposite market-cap regime.

proposals.notes: Pump event when OI is in its historical top decile for that asset (absolute level, not delta spike) with taker confirmation in high-liquidity coins — historically elevated OI means more participants exposed to a squeeze.
proposals.rationale: oi_pct_top_decile (cross-time percentile flag) is structurally different from oi_change_pct (short-term delta); no existing archetype uses the historical OI level flag as a primary condition paired with event_type=pump and taker confirmation.
